mission.title: linkerd: Classify some gRPC status codes as non-errors
mission.description: Classify some gRPC status codes as non-errors. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify linkerd troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your linkerd deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd -n linkerd --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review linkerd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant linkerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
kubectl get configmap -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=linkerd
```
## Feature Request

### What problem are you trying to solve?

Linkerd classifies all gRPC status codes as errors, other than `OK`.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Classify some gRPC status codes as non-errors
steps.description:
I'd like to propose that only error codes 13-15 be considered failures. From [the docs](https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/statuscodes.md):

Name | Code | Description
-- | -- | --
INTERNAL | 13 | Internal errors. This means that some invariants expected by the  underlying system have been broken. This error code is reserved for  serious errors.
UNAVAILABLE | 14 | The service is currently unavailable. This is most likely a  transient condition, which can be corrected by retrying with a

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm Classify some gRPC status codes as non-errors is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd -n linkerd --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n linkerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
I'd like to propose that only error codes 13-15 be considered failures. From [the docs](https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/statuscodes.md):

Name | Code | Description
-- | -- | --
INTERNAL | 13 | Internal errors. This means that some invariants expected by the  underlying system have been broken. This error code is reserved for  serious errors.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with linkerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
